Home | GitHub Source | Contact Me | Support the Site |

Main Series Home

Battle Tree Trainers

In the Battle Tree, enemy Trainers - their Trainer class and name - are pulled from a pre-existing list of 190 Trainers. Every Trainer in this list has a number, an IV value, and a Pokémon roster associated with them. The number determines when in their streak the player may face this trainer; lower numbers will appear in earlier battles than higher numbers. The IV value will determine what IVs the Pokémon belonging to this Trainer can possess. The enemy Trainer's three Pokémon will be a random selection of Pokémon from their roster, although they will never use two Pokémon of the same species or two Pokémon with the same held item.

If an enemy Trainer is scouted as a Multi Battle partner, they will use the first two Pokémon that were in their team when the player battled them. The Ability will be the same as the one that the player fought, but the gender will be set to male unless the Pokémon is genderless or female-only. There are also two partners that are not recruited by scouting them, Rada and Lillie; their rosters are shown below.

  • 1-30
  • 31-50
  • 51-70
  • 71-90
  • 91-110
  • 111-130
  • 131-190
  • Special Trainers
  • Multis Trainers

When doing the Normal Course, only Trainers 1-50, as well as Blue and Red, will ever be seen.

The numbers after the Pokémon's names refer to set numbers, explained in more detail on the Battle Tree Pokémon page.

Some Trainers can have different Pokémon between Sun & Moon and Ultra Sun & Ultra Moon. These Pokémon will have "(Sun/Moon only)" or "(Ultra Sun/Ultra Moon only)" below their name if this is the case. Pokémon that don't have such a note can be found in the roster in any of the four games.

Battle Tree Group Mechanics

Trainer groups each have a specific IV value associated with them. Pokémon that belong to a Trainer will receive the IVs of the Trainer's group. The IV values of the groups are given below:

  • 1-50: 19IV
  • 51-70: 23IV
  • 71-90: 27IV
  • 91-190: 31IV
  • Special trainers: 31IV

Generally speaking, earlier Trainers use Pokémon with lower set numbers than later Trainers. The exact progression of tiers and groups of Trainers is as follows:

Battle(s) Trainer Group(s) Pokémon Set(s)
1-9 (& 10 in Normal) 1-50 Normal Pokémon (Set-1)
11-19 31-70 Normal Pokémon (Set-1)
21-29 51-90 Normal Pokémon (Set-1 and Set-2)
31-39 71-110 Normal Pokémon (primarily Set-2 and Set-3, some Set-1)
41-49 91-130 Normal Pokémon (all sets)
51+ 91-190 Normal Pokémon & Legendary Pokémon (all sets)

Some additional resources:

  • The lookup tool linked here as "Battle Tree" allows you to enter a Trainer's name and their Pokémon and get information about the possible Pokémon you might face or are facing.

Special Trainers

Red (Normal Course)

Red appears as the 20th Trainer in the Singles Normal Course.

Red appears as the 50th Trainer in the Singles Super Course.

Multis Trainers

Rada

Rada is the default Multis Trainer before the player has scouted any other Trainers.

 Barbaracle-1
Hawlucha-1

Lillie

Lillie can only be recruited in Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon. She can be recruited by speaking to her at Professor Kukui's laboratory after finishing Episode RR.
